title: REVIEW SYMPOSIUM
abstract: Click to increase image sizeClick to decrease image size Acknowledgements The author would like to thank Dave Hill and Glenn Rikowski for their comments on earlier drafts of this paper. Professor Mike Cole, Bishop Grosseteste College, Lincoln LN1 3DY, UK. Email: Mike.Cole2@ntlworld.com Acknowledgement I would like to thank Sarah Smart and Katya Williams for discussing this review with me and Diane Reay for reading an earlier draft. Notes 1. Some of these problematics are standard critiques of the principle, and are recognised by Moore. The point is that Rikowski’s solution is a return to Marx, rather than the ‘refinement’ of sociological theory. 2. This is witnessed by a resurgence of Marxist theory in educational journals; for example, Journal for Critical Education Policy Studies (available free online at: http://www.jceps.com), Policy Futures in Education [although not a Marxist journal, it recently had to extend a proposed single issue on Marxism into a double issue (2004, 2(3&4)) because of the quantity and quality of contributions (available free online at: www.wwwords.co.uk/pfie)], the MERD conferences in Britain (http://www.flowideas.co.uk; search for ‘MERD’), and the Rouge Forum in the United States (www.pipeline.com/∼rgibson/rouge_forum). In addition, there are burgeoning developments in Central America (e.g. Fundacion McLaren) and South America (e.g. OLPED: www.lpp‐uerj.net/olped/).
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
